title: Particle Swarm Optimisation, with Focus on Environmental Models
text: hydroPSO R package global optimisation, parameter calibration, model evaluation using advanced variants Particle Swarm Optimisation (PSO). built scientists practitioners working hydrology, hydrometeorology, ecology, groundwater, environmental engineering, natural resources systems; especially calibration difficult, parameter spaces large, model runs computationally expensive. Unlike optimisation tools tied single model, hydroPSO model-independent. can connect : models written R external models executed system console workflows driven input output files legacy simulation tools whose source code want modify makes hydroPSO practical calibration engine real-world environmental modelling, reproducibility, transparency, flexibility matter much optimisation performance. title: Particle Swarm Optimisation, with Focus on Environmental Models
text: Environmental models rarely easy calibrate. often non-linear, non-smooth, computationally demanding. parameters can interact ways make local optimisation unreliable unstable. hydroPSO designed exactly kind problem.

title: Particle Swarm Optimisation, with Focus on Environmental Models
text: State---art PSO variants, including support SPSO-2011 SPSO-2007 Model-independent architecture, R-based externally executed models Parallel computing support reduce runtime expensive calibration tasks Sensitivity analysis tools diagnose parameter influence Graphical summaries diagnostics interpret optimisation behaviour results Fine control PSO settings search behaviour Reproducible workflow suited research, teaching, applied environmental modelling

text: file character, name (including path) output file values model / objective function particle iteration modelout.cols numeric, column number file store outputs read/plotted, without counting first three correspond iteration, particle goodness--fit value. modelout.cols=NULL, columns read, first three contains iteration number, particle number corresponding goodness--fit. nsim OPTIONAL. number simulated equivalent values model / objective function compared observations.  useful model calibrated returns NA instead simulated values parameter set(s) (e.g., MODFLOW). used force read_out read columns 4 4+nsim-1 file sim numeric zoo vector, simulated equivalent values model / objective function compared observations obs OPTIONAL. numeric zoo vector, observations compared best simulated value. obs provided, values read output ‘Observations.txt’ file results directory (default ‘PSO.’) obs.tzone OPTIONAL, relevant sub-daily observations. Time zone used reading observations ‘Observations.txt’ file results directory (default ‘PSO.’). obs.tzone provided, values read output ‘Observations.txt’ file using local time zone. dates OPTIONAL. character Date object used assign time stamps element sim obs. sim /obs already time stamp, -written dates  must length sim obs numeric zoo vectors MinMax OPTIONAL. character, indicates whether optimum value corresponds minimum maximum objective function. used filter model outputs non-acceptable performance Valid values : c('min', 'max') beh.thr OPTIONAL. numeric, used selecting behavioural parameter sets, .e. goodness--fit value larger/lower beh.th, depending value MinMax  used drawing horizontal line used separating behavioural non behavioural parameter sets verbose logical, TRUE, progress messages printed plot logical, indicates plot convergence measures produced ptype character, type plot. Valid values : -) corr: Scatterplot observed values best simulated counterpart. -) ts: possible observed values zoo type. graphical comparison observed values best simulated counterpart along time. requires hydroGOF package. See ggof. -) ecdf: Empirical CDFs computed plotted column sim. -) quant2ecdf: model output corresponding different parameter set (rows columns sim, according value byrow), different quantiles computed (many indicated quantiles.desired, Empirical CDFs computed plotted one previous quantiles). ftype OPTIONAL. used plot=TRUE ptype=="ts".See ggof FUN OPTIONAL. used plot=TRUE ptype=="ts".See ggof weights numeric vector, values weights used computing quantiles. title: Read and Visualise hydroPSO Result Files — ReadPlot_results
text: read_results reads information standard hydroPSO results directory, including parameter values, particle velocities, model outputs, convergence metrics, goodness--fit values. particular, reads following output files hydroPSO: 1) ‘BestParameterSet.txt’: best parameter set corresponding goodness--fit found optimisation 2) ‘Particles.txt’: parameter values corresponding goodness--fit value particles iterations 3) ‘Velocities.txt’: velocity values corresponding goodness--fit value particles iterations 4) ‘Model_out.txt’: values objective function/model output particle iteration 5) ‘ConvergenceMeasures.txt’: convergence measures summarizing performance hydroPSO 6) ‘Particles_GofPerIter.txt’: goodness--fit particles iterations plot_results high-level wrapper calls several lower-level plotting utilities sequence. Depending available files arguments, can produce: function plot_results high-level wrapper calls several lower-level plotting utilities sequence. Depending available files arguments, can produce: takes outputs read_results function produces following plots: 1) Dotty plots parameter values 2) Histograms parameter values 3) Boxplots parameter values 4) Correlation matrix among parameter values (optional) 5) Empirical CDFs parameter values 6) Parameter values vs Number Model Evaluations 7) (pseudo) 3D dotty plots (selected) parameter values 8) GoF particle Number Model Evaluations 9) Velocity values vs Number Model Evaluations  10a) Scatterplot Best Simulated values Observations (OPTIONAL, MinMax provided) 10b) Empirical CDFs model's output (produced obs zoo object) 10b) ggof (See ggof) Best Simulated values Observations (OPTIONAL, obs zoo object) 10d) Empirical CDFs selected quantiles model's output (OPTIONAL, obs zoo object) 11) Convergence Measures (Gbest normSwarmRadius) vs Iteration Number.

title: Read and Visualise hydroPSO Result Files — ReadPlot_results
text: read_results returns list following components: best.param: numeric vector best parameter set found optimisation, best.gof: numeric value best objective-function score, params: data frame tested parameter sets, gofs: numeric vector goodness--fit values associated params, velocities: matrix data frame particle velocities, model.values: model outputs read ‘Model_out.txt’, model.best: best simulated output identified according MinMax, model.obs: observed values used calibration, convergence.measures: convergence diagnostics read ‘ConvergenceMeasures.txt’, part.GofPerIter: goodness--fit values particles across iterations. plot_results invoked side effects. produces sequence diagnostic plots screen saves PNG files .png = TRUE. return structured object.

title: A flexible and model-independent Particle Swarm Optimisation (PSO) package for calibration/optimisation of environmental models — hydroPSO-package
text: hydroPSO implements state---art version Particle Swarm Optimisation (PSO) algorithm developed Kennedy Eberhart (1995) Eberhart Kennedy (1995). PSO population-based stochastic optimisation technique inspired social behaviour bird flocking, shares similarities evolutionary optimisation techniques Genetic Algorithms (GA). PSO, however, multi-dimensional solution space explored basis individual global best-known “particle positions” presence evolution operators. hydroPSO can used replacement 'optim' R function (global) optimization non-smooth non-linear functions. However, main focus hydroPSO calibration environmental real-world models need executed system console. hydroPSO model-independent, allowing user easily interface computer simulation model calibration engine (PSO). hydroPSO  communicates model model's input output files, without requiring access model's source code. principle, hydroPSO needs know “” model parameters need calibrated “” need written. , take control model(s) calibrated either maximum number iterations error tolerance reached: problem-specific user-defined. hydroPSO able take advantage multi-core machines network clusters alleviate computational burden complex models "long" execution time. hydroPSO includes sensitivity analysis, using Latin Hypercube One---Time (LH-OAT) method (van Griensven et al., 2006). addition, advanced plotting summaries detailed information evolution hydroPSO's performance facilitate interpretation assessment calibration results. time, hydroPSO features suite controlling options PSO variants fine-tune performance calibration engine model parameters sought, thus, allowing user customise different modelling problems. time, hydroPSO includes 4 different topologies (random, von Neumann, lbest, gbest), (non-)linear / random / adaptive / best-ratio inertia weight definitions (IW.type), time-variant acceleration coefficients (use.TVc1 use.TVc2), time-varying maximum velocity (use.TVlambda), regrouping strategy premature convergence detected (use.RG), options clamping maximal velocity (lambda), random LHS initialization positions velocities (Xini.type Vini.type), synchronous asynchronous update, 5 types boundary conditions (absorbing2011, absorbing2007, reflecting, damping, invisible) among others. default control arguments hydroPSO implements Standard PSO 2011 - SPSO2011 (see Clerc 2012; Clerc et al., 2010), although (better) settings recommeneded authors described Zambrano-Bigiarini & Rojas 2012.

title: Enhanced Particle Swarm Optimisation algorithm — hydroPSO
text: default hydroPSO function performs minimization fn, maximize fn MinMax='max' default control arguments hydroPSO implements Standard PSO 2011 - SPSO2011 (see Clerc 2012; Clerc et al., 2010). time, hydroPSO function provides options clamping maximal velocity, regrouping strategy premature convergence detected, time-variant acceleration coefficients, time-varying maximum velocity, (non-)linear / random / adaptive / best-ratio inertia weight definitions, random LHS initialization positions velocities, synchronous asynchronous update, 4 alternative neighbourhood topologies among others. control argument list can supply following components:  drty.OPTIONAL. Used fn='hydromod'  character, name directory storing input files required PSO, .e. ‘ParamRanges.txt’ ‘ParamFiles.txt’ drty.character, path directory storing output files generated hydroPSO param.ranges OPTIONAL. Used fn='hydromod' character, name file defining minimum maximum boundary values one parameters calibrated digits OPTIONAL. Used write2disk=TRUE numeric, number significant digits used writing output files scientific notation MinMax character, indicates whether maximization minimization problem needs solved. Valid values : c('min', 'max'). Default value min npart numeric, number particles swarm. default npart=NA, means swarm size depends value method: method='spso2007' npart=ceiling(10+2*sqrt(n)), npart=40 otherwise maxit numeric, maximum number iterations. default maxit=1000 maxfn numeric, maximum number function evaluations. Default value +Inf fn=='hydromod', stopping criterion uses number effective function calls, .e. function calls finite output value c1 numeric, cognitive acceleration coefficient. Encourages exploitation solution space reflects much particle influenced best-known position default c1= 0.5 + log(2) c2 numeric, social acceleration coefficient. Encourages exploration current global best reflects much particle influenced best-known optimum swarm default c2= 0.5 + log(2) use.IW logical, indicates inertia weight (w) used avoid swarm explosion, .e. particles flying around best position without converging (see Shi Eberhart, 1998) default use.IW=TRUE IW.w OPTIONAL. Used use.IW=TRUE IW.type!='GLratio' numeric, value inertia weight(s) (w [w.ini, w.fin]). can single number used iterations, can vector length 2 initial final values (order) w take along iterations default IW.w=1/(2*log(2)) use.CF logical, indicates Clerc's Constriction Factor (see Clerc, 1999; Eberhart Shi, 2000; Clerc Kennedy, 2002) used avoid swarm explosion default use.CF=FALSE lambda numeric [0,1], represents percentage limit maximum velocity (Vmax) dimension, computed vmax = lambda*(Xmax-Xmin) default lambda=1 abstol numeric, absolute convergence tolerance. algorithm stops gbest <= abstol (minimisation problems) gbest >= abstol (maximisation problems) default set -Inf +Inf minimisation maximisation problems, respectively reltol numeric, relative convergence tolerance. algorithm stops absolute difference best ‘personal best’ current iteration best ‘personal best’ previous iteration less equal reltol. Defaults sqrt(.Machine$double.eps), typically, 1e-8 reltol set 0, stopping criterion used Xini.type character, indicates initialise particles' positions swarm within ranges defined lower upper. Valid values : -) random: random initialisation positions within lower upper -) lhs: Latin Hypercube initialisation particle's positions, using npart number strata divide parameter range. requires lhs package. See randomLHS. -) sobol: Quasi-random initialisation particle's positions using uniform Sobol low discrepancy numbers within lower upper npart number observations extract. requires randtoolbox package. See sobol. default Xini.type='random' Vini.type character, indicates initialise particles' velocities swarm. Valid values : -) random2011: random initialisation velocities within lower-Xini upper-Xini, defined SPSO 2011 (Vini=U(lower-Xini, upper-Xini)) (see Clerc, 2012, 2010) -) lhs2011: random2011, using Latin Hypercube initialisation npart number strata instead random uniform distribution parameter. requires lhs package -) random2007: random initialisation velocities within lower upper using ‘half-diff’ method defined SPSO 2007 (Vini=[U(lower, upper)-Xini]/2) (see Clerc, 2012, 2010) -) lhs2007: random2007, using Latin Hypercube initialisation npart number strata instead random uniform distribution parameter. requires lhs package -) zero: particles initialised zero velocity default Vini.type=NA, means Vini.type depends value method: method='spso2007' Vini.type='random2007', Vini.type='random2011' otherwise best.update character, indicates () update global/neighbourhood personal best. Valid values : -)sync: update made synchronously, .e. computing position goodness--fit particles swarm. DEFAULT option -)async: update made asynchronously, .e. computing position goodness--fit individual particle swarm random.update OPTIONAL. used best.update='async' logical, TRUE particles processed random order update personal best global/neighbourhood best  default random.update=TRUE boundary.wall character, indicates type boundary condition applied optimisation. Valid values : NA, ‘absorbing2011’, ‘absorbing2007’, ‘reflecting’, ‘damping’, ‘invisible’ default boundary.wall=NA, means boundary.wall depends value method: method='spso2007' boundary.wall='absorbing2007', boundary.wall='absorbing2011' otherwise Experience shown Clerc's constriction factor inertia weights always confine particles within solution space. address problem, Robinson Rahmat-Samii (2004) Huang Mohan (2005) propose different boundary conditions, namely, reflecting, damping, absorbing invisible define particles treated reaching boundary searching space (see Robinson Rahmat-Samii (2004) Huang Mohan (2005) details) topology character, indicates neighbourhood topology used hydroPSO. Valid values c('random', 'gbest', 'lbest', 'vonNeumann'):  -) gbest: every particle connected , hence global best influences particles swarm. also termed star topology, generally assumed fast convergence vulnerable attraction sub-optimal solutions (see Kennedy, 1999; Kennedy Mendes, 2002, Schor et al., 2010) -) lbest: particle connected K immediate neighbours . also termed circles ring topology, generally swarm converge slower gbest topology less vulnerable sub-optimal solutions (see Kennedy, 1999; Kennedy Mendes, 2002) -) vonNeumann: particle connected K=4 immediate neighbours . topology densely connected lbest less densely gbest, thus, showing parallelism lbest benefiting bigger neighbourhood (see Kennedy Mendes, 2003) -) random: random topology special case lbest connections among particles randomly modified iteration showing improvement global best (see Clerc, 2005; Clerc, 2010) default topology='random'. K OPTIONAL. used topology c(random, lbest, vonNeumann)  numeric, neighbourhood size, .e. number informants particle (including particle ) considered computation personal best topology=lbest K MUST even number order consider amount neighbours left right particle  special case, K equal npart. default K=3 iter.ini OPTIONAL. used topology=='lbest'. default iter.ini=0. numeric, number iterations gbest topology used using lbest topology computation personal best particle option aims making faster identification global zone attraction. ngbest OPTIONAL. used method=='ipso' numeric, number particles considered computation global best default ngbest=4 (see Zhao, 2006) normalise logical, indicates whether parameter values normalised [0,1] interval optimisation . option appears C Matlab version SPSO-2011 (See https://www.particleswarm.info/standard_pso_2011_c.zip) recommended use option search space hypercube. search space hypercube, better normalise (small difference position without normalisation de-normalised one). default normalise=FALSE IW.type OPTIONAL. Used use.IW= TRUE length(IW.w)>1 character, defines inertia weight w vary along iterations. Valid values : -)linear: w varies linearly initial final values specified IW.w (see Shi Eberhart, 1998; Zheng et al., 2003). DEFAULT option -)non-linear: w varies non-linearly initial final values specified IW.w exponential factor IW.exp (see Chatterjee Siarry, 2006) -)runif: w uniform random variable range [w.min, w.max] specified IW.w. generalisation weight proposed Eberhart Shi (2001b) -)aiwf: adaptive inertia weight factor, inertia weight varied adaptively depending goodness--fit values particles (see Liu et al., 2005) -)GLratio: w varies according ratio global best average particle's local best (see Arumugam Rao, 2008) default IW.type='linear' IW.exp OPTIONAL. Used use.IW=TRUE IW.type='non-linear' numeric, non-linear modulation index (see Chatterjee Siarry, 2006) IW.type='linear', IW.exp set 1. default IW.exp=1 use.TVc1 logical, indicates cognitive acceleration coefficient c1 time-varying value instead constant one provided user (see Ratnaweera et al. 2004). default use.TVc1=FALSE TVc1.type character, required use.TVc1 = TRUE. Valid values : -)linear: c1 varies linearly initial final values specified TVc1.rng (see Ratnaweera et al., 2004) -)non-linear: c1 varies non-linearly initial final values specified TVc1.rng. Proposed authors hydroPSO taking account work Chatterjee Siarry (2006) inertia weight -)GLratio: c1 varies according ratio global best average particle's local best (see Arumugam Rao, 2008) default TVc1.type='linear' TVc1.rng OPTIONAL. Used use.TVc1=TRUE TVc1.type!='GLratio' numeric, initial final values cognitive acceleration coefficient [c1.ini, c1.fin] (order) along iterations default TVc1.rng=c(1.28, 1.05) TVc1.exp OPTIONAL. Used use.TVc1= TRUE TVc1.type= 'non-linear' numeric, non-linear modulation index TVc1.exp equal 1, TVc1 corresponds improvement proposed Ratnaweera et al., (2004), whereas TVc1.exp different one, reference found literature authors, included option based work Chatterjee Siarry (2006) inertia weight TVc1.type='linear', TVc1.exp automatically set 1. default TVc1.exp=1 use.TVc2 logical, indicates whether social acceleration coefficient c2 time-varying value constant one provided user (see Ratnaweera et al. 2004). default use.TVc2=FALSE TVc2.type character, required use.TVc2=TRUE. Valid values : -)linear: c2 varies linearly initial final values specified TVc2.rng (see Ratnaweera et al. 2004) -)non-linear: c2 varies non-linearly initial final values specified TVc2.rng. Proposed authors hydroPSO taking account work Chatterjee Siarry (2006) inertia weight default TVc2.type='linear' TVc2.rng OPTIONAL. Used use.TVc2=TRUE numeric, initial final values social acceleration coefficient [c2.ini, c2.fin] (order) along iterations default TVc2.rng=c(1.05, 1.28) TVc2.exp OPTIONAL. Used use.TVc2= TRUE TVc2.type='non-linear' numeric, non-linear modulation index TVc2.exp equal 1, TVc2 corresponds improvement proposed Ratnaweera et al., 2004, whereas TVc2.exp different one, reference found literature authors, included option based work Chatterjee Siarry (2006) inertia weight TVc2.type= linear, TVc2.exp automatically set 1. default TVc2.exp=1 use.TVlambda logical, indicates whether percentage limit maximum velocity lambda time-varying value constant value provided user. Proposed authors hydroPSO based work Chatterjee Siarry (2006) inertia weight default use.TVlambda=FALSE TVlambda.type character, required use.TVlambda=TRUE. Valid values : -)linear: TVvmax varies linearly initial final values specified TVlambda.rng -)non-linear: TVvmax varies non-linearly initial final values specified TVlambda.rng default TVlambda.type='linear' TVlambda.rng OPTIONAL. Used use.TVlambda=TRUE numeric, initial final values percentage limit maximum velocity [TVlambda.ini, TVlambda.fin] (order) along iterations default TVlambda.rng=c(1, 0.25) TVlambda.exp OPTIONAL. required use.TVlambda= TRUE TVlambda.type='non-linear' numeric, non-linear modulation index TVlambda.type='linear', TVlambda.exp automatically set 1. default TVlambda.exp=1 use.RG logical, indicates swarm regrouped premature convergence detected. default use.RG=FALSE use.RG=TRUE swarm regrouped search space centred around current global best. updated search space hoped small enough efficient search large enough allow swarm escape stagnation (see Evers Ghalia, 2009) 4 differences wrt Evers Ghalia 2009: -) swarm radius: median used instead max -) computation new range parameter space, corresponds boundaries whole swarm given iteration, instead maximum values ‘abs(x-Gbest)’ -) regrouping factor: RG.r instead ‘6/(5*ro)’ -) velocity re-initialized using Vini.type instead using formula proposed Evers Ghalia 2009 RG.thr required use.RG=TRUE numeric, positive number representing stagnation threshold used decide whether swarm regrouped . See Evers Galia (2009) details Regrouping occurs normalised swarm radius less RG.thr. default RG.thr=1E-5 RG.r required use.RG=TRUE. numeric, positive number representing regrouping factor, used regroup swarm search space centred around current global best (see Evers Galia, 2009 details). default RG.thr=2 RG.miniter required use.RG=TRUE numeric, minimum number iterations needed new regrouping. default RG.miniter=100 plot logical, indicates two-dimensional plot particles' position drawn iteration. high dimensional functions, first two dimensions particles plotted default plot=FALSE ..pbest logical, indicates best parameter values particle goodness--fit included output algorithm default ..pbest=FALSE ..fit.iter logical, indicates goodness--fit particle iteration included output algorithm default ..fit.iter=FALSE write2disk logical, indicates output files written disk. default write2disk=TRUE verbose logical, indicates progress messages printed. default verbose=TRUE REPORT OPTIONAL. Used verbose=TRUE  frequency report messages printed screen. Default every 100 iterations parallel character, indicates parallelise ‘hydroPSO’ (precise, evaluation objective function fn parallelised). Valid values : -)none: parallelisation made (default value) -)multicore: DEPRECATED!, since multicore package CRAN anymore. Originally thought carry parallel computations machines multiple cores CPUs. evaluation objective function fn done mclapply function parallel package. requires POSIX-compliant OS (essentially anything Windows) -)parallel: parallel computations network clusters machines multiple cores CPUs. ‘FORK’ cluster created makeForkCluster function.  fn.name="hydromod" evaluation objective function fn done clusterApply function parallel package. fn.name!="hydromod" evaluation objective function fn done parRapply function parallel package. -)parallelWin: parallel computations network clusters machines multiple cores CPUs (parallel implementation works Windows machines). ‘PSOCK’ cluster created makeCluster function. fn.name="hydromod" evaluation objective function fn done clusterApply function parallel package. fn.name!="hydromod" evaluation objective function fn done parRapply function parallel package. par.nnodes OPTIONAL. Used parallel!='none' numeric, indicates number cores/CPUs used local multi-core machine, number nodes used network cluster. default par.nnodes set amount cores detected function detectCores() (parallel package) par.pkgs OPTIONAL. Used parallel='parallelWin' list package names (characters) need loaded node allowing objective function fn evaluated par.env OPTIONAL. Used parallel!='none' environment objects required objective function fn exported parallel workers. default, environment hydroPSO called used par.export OPTIONAL. Used parallel!='none' character vector names additional objects exported par.env parallel workers. default, functions par.env exported

GOFcuts numeric vector defining breakpoints used convert gofs performance classes colouring panels. title: Test Functions for Global Optimisation — test_functions
text: Ackley test function multimodal separable, several local optima , search range [-32, 32], look like noise, although located regular intervals. Ackley function one global optimum located point o=(0,...,0). defined : $$ ackley = 20+\exp(1)-20\exp\left( -0.2\sqrt{\frac{1}{n}\sum_{=1}^{n}x_{}^2} \right)-\exp\left(\frac{1}{n}\sum_{=1}^{n}\cos(2\pi x_{})\right) ; -32 \leq x_i \leq 32 \ ; \ =1,2,\ldots,n $$ generalized Rastrigin test function non-convex, multimodal additively separable. several local optima arranged regular lattice, one global optimum located point o=(0,...,0). search range Rastrigin function [-5.12, 5.12] variable. function fairly difficult problem due large search space large number local minima. defined : $$ rastrigin = 10n+\sum_{=1}^{n}\left[x_{}^{2}-10\cos(2\pi x_{})\right] \ ; \ -5.12 \leq x_i \leq 5.12 \ ; \ =1,2,\ldots,n $$ Griewank test function multimodal non-separable, several local optima within search region defined [-600, 600]. similar Rastrigin function, number local optima larger case. one global optimum located point o=(0,...,0). function interpretation changes scale; general overview suggests convex function, medium-scale view suggests existence local minima, finally zoom details indicates complex structure numerous local minima. function exponentially increasing number local minima dimension increases, turns simple multistart algorithm able detect global minimum easily dimension increases (Locatelli, 2003). defined : $$ griewank = \frac{1}{4000}\sum_{=1}^{n}x_{}^{2}-\prod_{=1}^{n}\cos\left(\frac{x_i}{\sqrt{}}\right)+1 \ ; \ -600 \leq x_i \leq 600 \ ; \ =1,2,\ldots,n $$ Rosenbrock function non-convex, unimodal non-separable. also known Rosenbrock's valley Rosenbrock's banana function. global minimum inside long, narrow, parabolic shaped flat valley. find valley trivial. converge global minimum, however, difficult. one optimum located point o=(1,...,1). quadratic function, search range [-30, 30] variable. defined : $$ rosenbrock = \sum_{=1}^{n-1}\left[100(x_{+1}-x_{}^{2})^{2}+(1-x_{})^{2}\right] \ ; \ -30 \leq x_i \leq 30 \ ; \ =1,2,\ldots,n $$ main difficulty Schaffer's F6 test function size potential maxima need overcome get minimum increases closer one gets global minimum. defined : $$ schafferF6 = 0.5+\frac{\sin^{2}\sqrt{\sum_{=1}^{n}x_{}^{2}}-0.5}{(1+0.001\sum_{=1}^{n}x_{}^{2})^{2}} \ ; \ -100 \leq x_i \leq 100 \ ; \ =1,2,\ldots,n $$ first function De Jong's Sphere function one simple test functions available specialized literature. continuous, convex, unimodal additively separable test function can scaled number variables. belongs family functions called quadratic functions one optimum point o=(0,...,0). search range commonly used Sphere function [-100, 100] decision variable. defined : $$ sphere = \sum_{=1}^{n} x_{}^2 \ ; \ -100 \leq x_i \leq 100 \ ; \ =1,2,\ldots,n  $$ Schwefel's function non-convex, multimodal, additively separable. deceptive global minimum geometrically distant, parameter space, next best local minima. Therefore, search algorithms potentially prone convergence wrong direction. addition, less symmetric Rastrigin function global minimum edge search space [-500, 500] position o=(420.9687,...,420.9687). Additionally, overall, guiding slope towards global minimum like Ackley's, less extreme, Rastrigin's function. defined : $$ schwefel = 418.982887274338n + \sum_{=1}^{n} -x_{} \sin(\sqrt{|x_{}|} \ ; \ -500 \leq x_i \leq 500 \ ; \ =1,2,\ldots,n  $$ Shifted Schwefel's Problem 1.2 function unimodal, non-separable, scalable. defined : $$ sschwefel1\_2 = \sum_{=1}^{n} {  \left(\sum_{j=1}^{} {x_{j}} \right)^2 } + f\_bias \ ; \ -500 \leq x_i \leq 500 \ ; \ =1,2,\ldots,n  $$ optimisation algorithms take advantage known properties benchmark functions, local optima lying along coordinate axes, global optimum values many variables . order avoid previous shortcomings, shifting vector single bias introduced benchmark functions, reported afterwards. Shifted Ackley defined : $$ sackley = 20+\exp(1)-20\exp\left(-0.2\sqrt{\frac{1}{n}\sum_{=1}^{n}z_{}^2}\right)-\exp\left(\frac{1}{n}\sum_{=1}^{n}\cos(2\pi z_{})\right) + f\_bias , z=x-o ; \ =1,2,\ldots,n $$ Shifted Griewank defined : $$ sgriewank = \frac{1}{4000}\sum_{=1}^{n}z_{}^{2}-\prod_{=1}^{n}\cos\left(\frac{z_i}{\sqrt{}}\right)+1 + f\_bias \ , \ z=x-o ; \ =1,2,\ldots,n $$ Shifted Sphere defined : $$ ssphere = \sum_{=1}^{n} z_{}^2 + f\_bias \ , \ z=x-o ; \ =1,2,\ldots,n $$

code: o 'hydroPSO'     :                         -) now it allows to change parameters in three different ways:                            * 'replacement' (the old and default way in hydroPSO),                            * 'additive' (where the old value is summed up with the value computed during the optimisation), and                            * 'multiplicative' (where the old value is multiplied by the value computed during the optimisation).                            Changes were carried out in the 'ParameterValues2InputFiles.R' and 'ModifyInputFile.R' files.                         -) ParamRanges.txt file now supports three new columns: 'TypeChange', 'Min4Change', 'Max4Change',                            representing respectively the type of change to be made to each parameter (replacement, additive,                            multiplicative), the absolute minimum value that a parameter can take, and the absolute maximum value                            that a parameter can take. When a paramteter take a value outise the range [Min4Change, MaxChange],                            it is set to its closest boundary.                         -) 'param.ranges' argument is no longer passed in the 'control' argument' but in 'model.FUN.args' to be                            used directly by the 'hydromod' function.                         -) ParamFiles.txt file now supports one additional column 'RefValue', which is used as reference for                            additive or multiplicative changes.                         -) Now it is possible to initialise particle's positions with quasi-random Sobol sequences, by using                            \code{Xini.type='sobol'} within the 'Xini.type' element of the 'control' argument.                         -) Two new items within the 'control' argument in order to avoid modification of the .GlobalEnv variable                             when using PSOCK clusters (parallel == "parallelWin"), which require explicitly exporting objects.
